data_quality.assumption: Rows missing Latitude or Longitude cannot be geolocated and are dropped.
Rows missing Primary Type are dropped - the target variable cannot be imputed.
data_quality.impact: Included in the 74,115 dropped rows. Fabricating labels would invalidate the classifier.

data_quality.assumption: The single row missing Date is dropped - temporal features cannot be engineered without it.
Non-critical text columns (Description, Location Description, Block, FBI Code, IUCR) with nulls are filled with UNKNOWN to preserve the row.
data_quality.impact: 5,873 Location Description nulls filled. Row count unchanged.

data_quality.assumption: Ward (8.32% null) and Community Area (8.30% null) are filled with 0. These nulls are concentrated in older records (pre-2003) and are not random.
data_quality.impact: ~614k rows retain value 0 for Ward/Community Area. Models must treat 0 as a missing-indicator category.

geospatial.assumption: Any coordinate outside Chicago bounding box (Lat 41.60-42.05, Lon -88.00 to -87.50) is a data entry error.
Longitude IQR outliers [-87.84190, -87.50010] represent anomalous coordinate clusters within Chicago bounds and are removed.
geospatial.impact: 33,758 rows removed in ETL Step 9. Latitude had 0 outliers by IQR.

geospatial.assumption: Latitude and Longitude (WGS84) are used as spatial features. X/Y Coordinate (Illinois State Plane feet) are not used as features.
geospatial.impact: X/Y Coordinate columns retained in processed file but excluded from feature matrix.

temporal.assumption: All timestamps are in Chicago local time (CT, UTC-6 / UTC-5 DST). No timezone conversion applied.
temporal.impact: Hour-of-day features may be off by 1 hour during DST transitions (minor).

temporal.assumption: Records before 2001-01-01 or after today are data entry errors and are dropped.
temporal.impact: 0 rows removed - all records fell within 2001-2024.

target_variable.assumption: Primary Type is the classification target. It is standardized to UPPERCASE and rare types are merged.
target_variable.impact: 8 rare types (< 500 occurrences) merged into OTHER. Final: 29 classes.

target_variable.assumption: DOMESTIC VIOLENCE fell below the 500-row threshold and was merged into OTHER. While meaningful, it had insufficient samples for reliable classification.
target_variable.impact: DOMESTIC VIOLENCE records are now labeled OTHER. Threshold can be lowered to 100 to preserve this class if needed.

modelling.assumption: Arrest is EXCLUDED from model features. It is a post-incident field - knowing whether an arrest was made would constitute target leakage for a pre-incident prediction system.
modelling.impact: Arrest column retained in processed file but must not be passed to the classifier.

modelling.assumption: Case Number deduplication keeps the first occurrence. Updated records (e.g. arrest status changes) reflect administrative updates, not new crimes.
modelling.impact: 565 updated records removed. First-reported state of each crime is the prediction target.

modelling.assumption: Time-series cross-validation with temporal splits will be used in Phase 2 to prevent future data leaking into training.
modelling.impact: Train on years 2001-2020, validate on 2021-2022, test on 2023-2024 (subject to Phase 2 design).

ethics_and_fairness.assumption: The model predicts crime TYPE, not individuals. No demographic or personal data is used as a feature.
Geographic features (Latitude, Longitude, Community Area) may encode historical policing bias. Over-policed areas appear to have more recorded crime.
ethics_and_fairness.impact: Model predictions reflect recorded crime patterns, not necessarily true crime rates. This limitation must be stated in deployment documentation.
